title: DebugDumper WS entry types — ws_send and ws_lifecycle
purpose: Extend the DebugDumper with entry types for WebSocket traffic so that WS transport debug output uses the same JSONL structure as HTTP. Outgoing WS frames get a ws_send entry type, connection lifecycle events get a ws_lifecycle entry type, and incoming WS events reuse the existing sse_event type via SseEvent mapping (D3).
task: Add two new DebugPayload variants (WsSend and WsLifecycle) and corresponding write methods (write_ws_send and write_ws_lifecycle) to the DebugDumper. Incoming WS events need no new code — they are mapped to SseEvent and logged via the existing write_sse_event method.

requirements.title: WsSend debug payload variant
requirements.spec: The DebugPayload enum SHALL include a WsSend variant with message_type (String) and body (serde_json::Value) fields. The entry type discriminator SHALL be ws_send.
acceptance: DebugPayload::WsSend variant exists with message_type and body fields
Serializes to JSONL with type: ws_send

requirements.title: WsLifecycle debug payload variant
requirements.spec: The DebugPayload enum SHALL include a WsLifecycle variant with event (String — connect/reconnect/close), url (String), and reason (Option<String>) fields. The entry type discriminator SHALL be ws_lifecycle.
acceptance: DebugPayload::WsLifecycle variant exists with event, url, and reason fields
Serializes to JSONL with type: ws_lifecycle
reason is omitted from JSON when None

requirements.title: write_ws_send and write_ws_lifecycle methods
requirements.spec: DebugDumper SHALL expose pub fn write_ws_send(&self, message_type: &str, body: &serde_json::Value) and pub fn write_ws_lifecycle(&self, event: &str, url: &str, reason: Option<&str>) methods that append the corresponding JSONL entries.
acceptance: write_ws_send appends a ws_send entry with timestamp, message_type, and body
write_ws_lifecycle appends a ws_lifecycle entry with timestamp, event, url, and optional reason
Both methods use the same append_entry infrastructure as existing methods

requirements.title: Incoming WS events reuse write_sse_event
requirements.spec: Incoming WS messages SHALL be logged via the existing write_sse_event method after being mapped to SseEvent. No new DebugDumper method is needed for incoming WS events.
acceptance: No new method added for incoming WS events
Design doc (D3) confirms SseEvent mapping makes write_sse_event applicable to WS

boundaries: SHALL NOT modify existing DebugPayload variants (Request, ResponseMeta, SseEvent)
SHALL NOT modify the write_request, write_response_meta, or write_sse_event methods
SHALL NOT add WebSocket transport code — this brief only extends the debug infrastructure
verification: New tests verify ws_send and ws_lifecycle JSONL entries serialize correctly
Existing debug dump tests pass unchanged
